title: SSC CGL T-I Reasoning Section Test

questions.question: Apply the same pattern used in the following group and find the correct option. NUMBER : ERBMUN :: SCHOOL : ?
questions.explanation: Given: NUMBER : ERBMUN :: SCHOOL : ? Logic: Positions of letters are interchanged. For, NUMBER : ERBMUN Similarly, SCHOOL : ? So, SCHOOL : OLOHCS Thus, correct option is (b).

questions.question: Find the number that breaks the pattern: 3, 7, 15, 31, 63, 120 ​​
questions.explanation: Given: 3, 7, 15, 31, 63, 120 ​​ Logic: Numbers are multiply by 2 and + 1 place. 3 × 2 + 1 = 7 7 × 2 + 1 = 15 15 × 2 + 1 = 31 31 × 2 + 1 = 63 63 × 2 + 1 ≠\neq= 120 So, 120 is breaks the pattern. Thus, correct option is (d).

questions.question: Statement: Online education requires reliable internet access. Assumptions: I. Students have access to the internet. II. Traditional education is outdated. ​​​​
options: Both I and II are implicit
Neither I nor II is implicit
questions.explanation: Statement: Online education requires reliable internet access. Assumptions: I. Students have access to the internet. This is implicit, because without internet access, online education cannot function. II. Traditional education is outdated. ​​ This is not implicit, the statement says nothing about traditional education. So, Only I is implicit. Thus, correct option is (a).

questions.question: Statement: Most students in the class passed the exam. Conclusions: I. Some students failed the exam. II. All students appeared for the exam.
questions.explanation: Statement: Most students in the class passed the exam. Conclusions: I. Some students failed the exam. Follows, “most” means more than half, but not all so at least some students did not pass (i.e., failed). II. All students appeared for the exam. Not follow, the statement does not say that all students appeared; it only talks about those who passed. So, Only I follows. Thus, correct option is (a).

questions.question: ​Select the letter-cluster from among the given options that can replace the question mark (?) in the following series. EHF, JKM, ONT, ?
questions.explanation: Given: EHF, JKM, ONT, ? Logic: 1st letter + 5, 2nd letter + 3 and 3rd letter + 7 place. For, 1st letter E + 5 = J, J + 5 = O, O + 5 = T For, 2nd letter H + 3 = K, K + 3 = N, N + 3 = Q For, 3rd letter F + 7 = M, M + 7 = T, T + 7 = A So, the missing term is TQA. Thus, correct option is (c).

questions.question: If a + b = 20 and a - b = 6, then what is a × b?
questions.explanation: Given: If a + b = 20 and a - b = 6 Solution: Add both equations: 2a = 26 =&gt a = 13 Substitute a = 13 into a + b = 20: b = 7 Now, a × b = 13 × 7 = 91 So, a × b = 91 Thus, correct option is (d).

questions.question: A man says, “She is the wife of my father’s only son.” How is the woman related to the man?
questions.explanation: Given: A man says, “She is the wife of my father’s only son.” From the given information blood relation diagram will be. So, the woman is wife of the man. Thus, correct option is (c).

questions.question: ​Select the letter-cluster from among the given options that can replace the question mark (?) in the following series. BDH, EJM, IPR, ?
questions.explanation: Given: BDH, EJM, IPR, ? Logic: 1st letter increasing from 3, 2nd letter + 6 and 3rd letter + 5 place. For, 1st letter B + 3 = E, E + 4 = I, I + 5 = N For, 2nd letter D + 6 = J, J + 6 = P, P + 6 = V For, 3rd letter H + 5 = M, M + 5 = R, R + 5 = W So, the missing term is NVW. Thus, correct option is (a).

questions.question: In a row of 60 students, P is 18th from the left and Q is 25th from the right. How many students are between P and Q?
questions.explanation: Given: In a row of 60 students, P is 18th from the left and Q is 25th from the right. Solution: Total students = 60 P is 18th from the left. Q is 25th from the right, so Q’s position from the left: = 60 - 25 + 1 = 36 Now positions from the left: P = 18 and Q = 36 Students between P and Q: = 36 - 18 - 1 = 17 So, 17 students are between P and Q. Thus, correct option is (c).

questions.question: Find the odd one out. 729, 1331, 2197, 3125
questions.explanation: Given: 729, 1331, 2197, 3125 729 = 93​ 1331 = 113 2197 = 133​ 3125 = 55​ 729, 1331 and 2197 are cubes of consecutive odd numbers. So, 3125 does not follow the pattern. Thus, correct option is (d).

questions.question: If X is the daughter of Y and Y is the brother of Z, how is X related to Z?
questions.explanation: Given: If X is the daughter of Y and Y is the brother of Z. From the given information blood relation diagram will be. So, X is the niece of Z. Thus, correct option is (a).

questions.question: Apply the same pattern used in the following group and find the correct option. KLM : NOP :: STU : ?
questions.explanation: Given: KLM : NOP :: STU : ? Logic: Letters are increasing + 3 place. For, KLM : NOP K + 3 = N, L + 3 = O, M + 3 = P Similarly, STU : ? S + 3 = V, T + 3 = W, U + 3 = X So, STU : VWX Thus, correct option is (d).

questions.question: If 8 + 4 = 96 and 7 + 3 = 70, then 6 + 5 = ?
questions.explanation: Given: If 8 + 4 = 96 and 7 + 3 = 70 Logic: (1st number × 2nd number) + (1st number)2= 3rd number For, 8 + 4 = 96 (8 × 4) + (8)2= 32 + 64 = 96 For, 7 + 3 = 70 (7 × 3) + (7)2= 21 + 49 = 70​ Similarly, 6 + 5 = ? (6 × 5) + (6)2= 30 + 36 = 66​ Thus, correct option is (b).

questions.question: How many letters are between the 1st from left and 2nd from right in the word COMPETITION, when arranged alphabetically?
questions.explanation: Given: COMPETITION Given Letters C O M P E T I T I O N Alphabetical Order C E I I M N O O P T T Alphabetical order: C E I I M N O O P T T 1st letter from left: C 2nd letter from right: T Letter are between 1st from left and 2nd from right: E, I, I, M, N, O, O, P So, 8 letters are between the 1st from left and 2nd from right in the word. Thus, correct option is (b).

questions.question: Find the odd one out. Sodium, Calcium, Iron, Oxygen
questions.explanation: Sodium, Calcium and Iron are metals. Oxygen is non-metal. So, oxygen is odd one out. Thus, correct option is (b).

questions.question: If the sum of 36 and 24 is multiplied by 7, what is the result?
questions.explanation: Given: If the sum of 36 and 24 is multiplied by 7 Solution: 36 + 24 = 60 Now multiply by 7: 60 × 7 = 420 So, the result is 420. Thus, correct option is (a).

questions.question: Find the missing term in the series. ?, 132, 165, 204, 249
questions.explanation: Given: ?, 132, 165, 204, 249 Logic: Double difference increasing + 6 place. 165 - 132 = 33 204 - 165 = 39 249 - 204 = 45 Differences are increasing by + 6. 33, 39, 45 The previous difference should be: 33 - 6 = 27 132 - 27 = 105 So, the missing term is 105. Thus, correct option is (b).

questions.question: Which of the following addresses are exactly identical? Amit Verma, 12A, Green Park, Delhi, 110016 Amit Verma, 12A Green Park, Delhi, 110016 Amit Verma, 12A, Green Park, Delhi, 110018 Amit Verma, 12A, Green Park, Delhi, 110016 ​
questions.explanation: Given: Amit Verma, 12A, Green Park, Delhi, 110016 Amit Verma, 12A Green Park, Delhi, 110016 Amit Verma, 12A, Green Park, Delhi, 110018 Amit Verma, 12A, Green Park, Delhi, 110016 ​ Compare the addresses carefully: 1. Amit Verma, 12A, Green Park, Delhi, 110016 2. Amit Verma, 12A Green Park, Delhi, 110016 Same details; comma difference doesn’t matter 3. Amit Verma, 12A, Green Park, Delhi, 110018 PIN code is different 4. Amit Verma, 12A, Green Park, Delhi, 110016 Exactly same as Address 1 So, the exactly identical addresses are 1 and 4. Thus, correct option is (b).

questions.question: Find the missing term in the series. 2, 6, 18, 54, ?
questions.explanation: Given: 2, 6, 18, 54, ? Logic: Numbers are multiply by 3. 2 × 3 = 6 6 × 3 = 18 18 × 3 = 54 54 × 3 = 162 So, the missing term is 162. Thus, correct option is (c).

questions.question: If BALL = 211212 and CALL = 311212, then DOLL = ?
questions.explanation: Given: If BALL = 211212 and CALL = 311212 Logic: Place value of letters are given. For, BALL = 211212 B = 2, A = 1, L = 12, L = 12 For, CALL = 311212 C = 3, A = 1, L = 12, L = 12 Similarly, DOLL = ? D = 4, O = 15, L = 12, L = 12 So, DOLL = 4151212 Thus, correct option is (d).

questions.question: Pointing to a boy, a woman said, “He is the son of my mother’s only son.” How is the boy related to the woman?
questions.explanation: Given: Pointing to a boy, a woman said, “He is the son of my mother’s only son.” From the given information blood relation diagram will be. So, the boy is nephew of the woman. Thus, correct option is (d).

questions.question: How many meaningful 4-letter English words can be formed using 2nd, 3rd, 4th and 5th letters of the word TEACHER, without repetition?
questions.explanation: Given Word: TEACHER Letters by position: 1-T, 2-E, 3-A, 4-C, 5-H, 6-E, 7-R Required letters → 2nd, 3rd, 4th, 5th The letters are: E, A, C, H Now form meaningful 4-letter English words using these letters without repetition. Possible meaningful words: ACHE EACH Total meaningful words = 2 So, two meaningful 4-letter English words can be formed using 2nd, 3rd, 4th and 5th letters of the word TEACHER, without repetition. Thus, correct option is (a).

questions.question: In the following question, select the related word from the given alternatives. Freedom : Independence :: Poverty : ?
questions.explanation: Given: Freedom : Independence :: Poverty : ? Freedom leads to independence. Similarly, Poverty : ? Poverty leads to hardship. Thus, correct option is (c).

questions.question: Two numbers are in the ratio 5 : 7 and their sum is 144. Find the numbers.
questions.explanation: Given: Two numbers are in the ratio 5 : 7 and their sum is 144. Solution: Let the numbers be 5x and 7x. 5x + 7x = 144 12x = 144 x = 12 The numbers are: 5x = 5 × 12 = 60 7x = 7 × 12 = 84 So, the numbers are 60, 84. Thus, correct option is (b).

questions.question: In the following question, select the related word from the given alternatives. Cell : Tissue :: Tissue : ?
questions.explanation: Given: Cell : Tissue :: Tissue : ? Cells combine to form tissue. Similarly, Tissue : ? Tissues combine to form an organ. Thus, correct option is (c).
